data.title: Horizon Europe separates from First Brands Group, unaffected by U.S. operations wind down involving Brake Parts Inc., Cardone, Autolite.
data.description: A company in Germany known as Horizon Europe, a prominent manufacturer of branded towing and trailering equipment globally, recently issued a statement in response to the announcement made by First Brands Group, LLC. First Brands declared the systematic closure of some of its North American operations, such as Brake Parts Inc., Cardone, and Autolite. However, Horizon Europe confirmed that it is currently in the process of disassociating from First Brands and asserted that its operations remain unaffected by the ongoing business adjustments within the U.S. The specifics of the impact on Frist Brands were not mentioned, but Horizon Europe reassured its continuity amidst these changes.

data.title: WIF price set to recover to $0.42-$0.43 by February after technical consolidation. Short-term target at $0.35-$0.36.
data.description: Summary of WIF Price Prediction: In the short term (1 week), the target price range is between $0.35 to $0.36. data.title: CBL Properties shares 2025 tax reporting info; Ordinary Dividends: $0.50 per share, Capital Gain Distribution: TBD.
data.description: CBL Properties revealed tax information for the 2025 distributions on its Common stock (NYSE:CBL). Each share received distributions in two categories: Ordinary Dividends and Capital Gain Distribution. The Record Date and Payable Date for these distributions were provided for shareholders' reference. The company allocated the 2025 Ordinary Dividends to Box 1a on Form 1099-DIV, while the 2025 Capital Gain Distribution was detailed separately.This was specific to CBL & Associates Properties, Inc., Common Stock with CUSIP number 124830-878 and listed on NYSE under the symbol CBL. Shareholders were advised to refer to this information for accurate tax reporting purposes.

data.title: Crypto funds saw $1.73B in outflows last week, largest since November, as Bitcoin, Ethereum ETFs bleed. U.S.-led redemptions intensified.
data.description: Investors pulled out $1.73 billion from digital asset investment products last week as redemptions led by the United States increased.

data.title: SLB's Q4 revenue hits $9.745B, up 5% from the year prior, exceeding analyst expectations. EPS at 78 cents; outlook optimistic.
data.description: SLB Limited (NYSE: SLB) posted its fourth-quarter results, indicating a sequential improvement as global upstream activity stabilized. The company reported fourth-quarter revenue of $9.745 billion, a 5% increase from the previous year and surpassing the analyst estimate of $9.547 billion. Diluted GAAP earnings per share were 55 cents, down from 77 cents year over year. Additionally, diluted EPS, excluding charges and credits, stood at 78 cents, a decline from 92 cents in the previous year but beating the 74-cent estimate. The CEO mentioned the challenges faced in 2025 due to lower commodity prices, geopolitical uncertainty, and an oversupplied oil market. To address these challenges, the company focused on enhancing resilience by accelerating strategies such as emphasizing production and recovery, increasing the deployment of AI solutions, and rapidly expanding the Data Center Solutions business. For further details, refer to the full story on Benzinga.com.

data.title: First Brands Group starts wind down of Brake Parts Inc., Cardone, and Autolite units in North America. Assessing transition options.
data.description: A prominent automotive parts supplier, First Brands Group, LLC, based in Rochester Hills, Michigan, has recently disclosed the start of winding down specific business operations in North America. This includes well-known brands like Brake Parts Inc., Cardone, and Autolite. The company is actively working to transition these units to new ownership while its other business segments remain operational. First Brands Group, LLC is focusing on evaluating various options for the future following a thorough assessment in recent months.

data.title: Gray showcasing design-build expertise at the 2026 IPPE in Atlanta, will engage with customers and partners in the industry.
data.description: In anticipation of the 2026 International Production & Processing Expo (IPPE) in Atlanta, the global protein and food processing industry is gearing up to convene for the event. Gray is eager to engage with customers, partners, and key figures in the industry during the expo. The expo will take place in Atlanta, GA, from January 26 to January 28, 2026.

data.title: InMode's stock soars on rumored $1.1B takeover bid by foreign fund, hitting three-month high. via Investor's Business Daily.
data.description: An analyst suggested that a foreign investment fund may acquire the medical aesthetics outlet InMode for a staggering $1.1 billion. This speculation caused a surge in InMode's stock on Monday, propelling it to a three-month high. This potential takeover offer has attracted significant attention and interest in the market.

data.title: Inventor from Indianapolis develops 2 STEP exerciser for full body workout. (SGM-608) - PITTSBURGH, Jan. 26, 2026.
data.description: On January 26, 2026 in Pittsburgh, an inventor from Indianapolis, Indiana, shared their creation of the 2 STEP exercise equipment. The inventor expressed the idea behind the product, aiming to provide a means to work out the core, upper body, and lower body all with one piece of equipment. The innovative design of the 2 STEP allows users to participate in a range of exercises targeting different muscle groups efficiently. This invention marks a new approach to fitness equipment, consolidating multiple workout benefits into a single apparatus.

data.title: UPS likely to report Q4 earnings at $2.2/share, down from $2.75/share; revenue expected at $24.01B vs $25.3B last year. UPS stock fell 1.2%.
data.description: United Parcel Service, Inc. will announce fourth-quarter earnings on January 27 before the market opens. Analysts predict earnings of $2.2 per share, a decrease from $2.75 per share last year. Revenue is anticipated to be $24.01 billion, down from $25.3 billion in the previous year. In the third quarter, UPS surpassed expectations and provided fourth-quarter sales guidance exceeding estimates. The company's stock price closed at $107.98, decreasing by 1.2% on Friday. For further details and analyst ratings, visit the Analyst Stock Ratings page on Benzinga.

data.title: QCR Hldgs to release quarterly earnings on 2026-01-27, estimated EPS of $1.98. Expectations high for surpassing estimates.
data.description: QCR Holdings (NASDAQ: QCRH) is set to unveil its quarterly earnings report on Tuesday, January 27, 2026. Analysts are forecasting an earnings per share (EPS) of $1.98 for the company. Investors are eagerly awaiting the announcement, with optimism surrounding the possibility of QCR Holdings exceeding expectations and offering a positive outlook for the upcoming quarter. It's crucial for new investors to recognize that guidance provided by the company can greatly influence stock prices. In the previous quarter, QCR Holdings outperformed expectations in terms of EPS.
